---
title: "Using cositos"
---

> **Who this section is for:** notebook authors who want to *use* interactive widgets —
> driven from Python, Julia, R, or Clojure. **We assume** you know a notebook language and
> have used Jupyter. **We do not assume** you know [anywidget](https://anywidget.dev),
> ipywidgets, or any JavaScript. New terms are defined in the
> [**Glossary**](glossary.qmd) and linked on first use.

## Setup

```bash
pip install cositos anywidget
```

`cositos` runs in your **kernel** (the notebook's language engine — the thing running your
Python/Julia). `anywidget` supplies the **frontend** (the JavaScript that renders the
widget in the browser/JupyterLab). You need both for a *live* widget; for static HTML
export you need only `cositos`. See the [Glossary](glossary.qmd) for kernel vs frontend.

## Learn by doing (Tutorials)

1. [**Quickstart**](quickstart.qmd) — run your first widget in Python, Julia, or Clojure.
2. [**Author a widget**](authoring-widgets.qmd) — write your own widget's ESM frontend.
3. [**Save & restore**](save-restore.qmd) — persist widget state to JSON and reload it.
4. [**Build a dashboard**](dashboard.qmd) — compose several widgets through one state model.
5. [**Static HTML export**](static-export.qmd) — ship widgets with no live kernel.
6. [**Integrate a plotting library**](plot-integration.qmd) — Plotly / Altair / bqplot.
7. [**On the web (Observable JS)**](web-ojs.qmd) — render widgets fully in the browser.
8. [**Integrate into a host**](integrating.qmd) — the Transport/Channel seam per environment.

## Look it up (Reference)

- [**API reference**](api-reference.qmd) — every public symbol and signature.
- [**API cheat sheet**](api-cheatsheet.qmd) — cross-language name lookup.
- [**Widget gallery**](widgets-gallery.qmd) — live demos + launch snippets.
- [**Widget categories**](widgets.md) — what kinds of widgets cositos can express.
- [**Hosts & channels**](hosts.md) — per-host support (Jupyter, Clay, Pluto, …).
- [**Implementation status**](status.md) — languages and hosts supported today.

## Understand it (Explanation)

- [**State discipline**](state-discipline.qmd) — keeping many widgets consistent as your
  app grows (read this once your app has more than a handful of widgets).
